hunt.title: The Cambridge Hunt
hunt.description: Race through the heart of Cambridge, from ancient colleges to modern landmarks. 5 locations, each more challenging to find than the last.

clues.hard: A monarch's offering to the divine, where voices have risen in song for over five centuries. Stone fingers reach for the heavens.
clues.medium: This perpendicular Gothic masterpiece was founded by Henry VI in 1446. Its fan vault ceiling is the world's largest.
clues.easy: King's College Chapel on King's Parade — stand at the front gate facing the Great Lawn.

evidenceChallenge.instruction: Take a photo of the inscription above the main entrance.
evidenceChallenge.question: What is the third word of the inscription?

clues.hard: Tangents and arcs made timber, this crossing was supposedly assembled without fasteners — or so the legend goes.
clues.medium: A wooden footbridge at Queens' College, designed by William Etheridge in 1749, often wrongly attributed to Newton.
clues.easy: The Mathematical Bridge at Queens' College — view it from Silver Street Bridge over the River Cam.

evidenceChallenge.question: What year was the current bridge built?

clues.hard: Crusaders carried this shape home from the Holy Sepulchre. Only four of its kind survive in England.
clues.medium: The Church of the Holy Sepulchre, dating to 1130, is one of England's few remaining round Norman churches.
clues.easy: The Round Church on the corner of Bridge Street and Round Church Street — one of Cambridge's oldest buildings.

evidenceChallenge.instruction: Take a photo of the memorial stone by the entrance.
evidenceChallenge.question: What name appears first on the memorial stone?

clues.hard: In this establishment, the secret of life was first announced over a pint in February 1953. The ceiling still bears wartime signatures.
clues.medium: Watson and Crick announced the discovery of the structure of DNA here. RAF and USAAF airmen left graffiti on the ceiling during WWII.
clues.easy: The Eagle Pub on Bene't Street — stand at the main entrance beneath the hanging pub sign.

evidenceChallenge.instruction: Photograph the blue DNA plaque outside the pub.
evidenceChallenge.question: What is the sixth word on the plaque?

clues.hard: A gilded insect devours the minutes atop a face of concentric rings. Time is displayed but never accurately — by design.
clues.medium: The Chronophage (time-eater), a large grasshopper-like sculpture, sits atop John C. Taylor's gold-plated clock unveiled by Stephen Hawking in 2008.
clues.easy: The Corpus Clock on the corner of Bene't Street and Trumpington Street, outside the Taylor Library of Corpus Christi College.

evidenceChallenge.instruction: Take a photo of the dedication plaque beneath the clock.
evidenceChallenge.question: Who is credited as the designer?
